data.title: Merlin Properties Reports Strong Earnings and Growth
data.description:
Merlin Properties (OTC) ( (MRPRF) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Merlin Properties (OTC) presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Merlin Properties (OTC) is a leading real estate company operating in the office, logistics, and shopping center sectors, known for its strategic investments and high occupancy rates across its portfolio.

In its latest earnings report, Merlin Properties demonstrated a strong financial performance with significant increases in net earnings and gross rents, alongside a stable and high occupancy rate across its properties.

The company reported total revenues of €413 million, marking a 7.7% year-over-year increase. Gross rents rose by 6.6% to €398.1 million, with net earnings experiencing a substantial rise of 158.8% to €583.1 million. The company’s occupancy rate remained high at 95.5%, showcasing its robust leasing activity across various sectors, including offices, logistics, and shopping centers.

Merlin Properties continues to focus on strategic developments, including the successful deployment of its Mega Plan and the expansion of its data centers. The company also issued a €550 million green bond, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable growth.

Looking ahead, Merlin Properties remains optimistic about its growth prospects, with ongoing investments in its logistics and data center developments expected to drive future revenue and occupancy gains. The management’s forward-looking strategy aims to maintain high occupancy levels and enhance shareholder value.

data.title: Bavarian Nordic Reports Strong Revenue Growth in 2025
data.description:
Bavarian Nordic A/S ( (BVNRY) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Bavarian Nordic A/S presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Bavarian Nordic A/S is a global vaccine company specializing in the development and production of vaccines for infectious diseases, with a strong focus on travel health and public preparedness sectors.

Bavarian Nordic has reported a robust financial performance for the first nine months of 2025, with a 32% increase in revenue to DKK 4,793 million, driven by strong demand in both its Travel Health and Public Preparedness segments. The company also highlighted significant progress in the global launch of its chikungunya vaccine, Vimkunya, and the completion of a lucrative sale of a Priority Review Voucher.

Key financial metrics include a 23% increase in Travel Health revenue, primarily due to rabies and tick-borne encephalitis vaccines, and a notable 51% growth in Public Preparedness revenue. The company’s EBITDA before special items reached DKK 1,477 million, with a margin of 31%. Strategic achievements include the expansion of Vimkunya’s market presence across Europe and securing a substantial contract for smallpox/mpox vaccines with the European Commission.

Looking ahead, Bavarian Nordic remains optimistic about meeting its financial targets for 2025, with expected total revenue of approximately DKK 6,000 million and an EBITDA margin of around 26% before special items. The company is poised for continued growth, supported by secured contracts and strategic partnerships, as it navigates the evolving landscape of global health preparedness.

data.title: AF Gruppen ASA Reports Record Third-Quarter Results
data.description:
AF Gruppen ASA Class A ( (AGRUF) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information AF Gruppen ASA Class A presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

AF Gruppen ASA is a leading Norwegian construction and civil engineering company, known for its innovative approach and commitment to sustainability across various sectors, including civil engineering, construction, offshore, and energy. In its third-quarter report for 2025, AF Gruppen ASA announced its best-ever quarterly results, highlighting strong performance across most business areas, robust cash flow, and a solid order backlog. The company reported revenues of NOK 7,803 million for the quarter, with earnings before tax reaching NOK 398 million, reflecting a profit margin of 5.1%. The order backlog increased to NOK 44,382 million, and the company declared an additional dividend of NOK 4.00 per share. Key highlights include significant revenue growth in the Civil Engineering sector, with new contracts such as the carbon capture facility at Hafslund Celsio’s plant, and strong performance in the Construction sector with multiple new contracts. The Energy and Environment sector saw a strategic acquisition and new contracts, while the Offshore sector secured a landmark decommissioning contract. Looking ahead, AF Gruppen’s management remains optimistic, focusing on strategic growth and sustainability initiatives, despite macroeconomic challenges and high interest rates. The company aims to leverage its strong order backlog and innovative capabilities to maintain its growth trajectory.

data.title: BioLargo Reports Q3 2025 Financial Results Amid Challenges
data.description:
BioLargo ( (BLGO) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information BioLargo presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

BioLargo, Inc. is a Delaware-based company that invents, develops, and commercializes innovative platform technologies aimed at solving environmental challenges such as PFAS contamination, water treatment, and odor control. The company operates in the environmental technology sector and leverages its scientific and engineering expertise to bring disruptive technologies to market.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, BioLargo reported a significant net loss, primarily due to a credit loss expense from a customer’s contractual default. Despite this setback, the company continues to focus on its strategic initiatives and technological advancements.

Key financial highlights include a total revenue of $7.1 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, a decrease from the previous year. The company faced an operating loss of $10.8 million, with a notable credit loss expense of $3.8 million due to a customer’s default. BioLargo’s cash and cash equivalents increased to $4.5 million, supported by financing activities that provided $7.3 million in net cash.

Looking forward, BioLargo’s management remains focused on increasing revenues and securing additional financing to support its operations and technological investments. The company acknowledges the challenges ahead but is committed to leveraging its innovative solutions to drive future growth and market presence.

data.title: Biotricity Inc. Reports Revenue Growth Amid Financial Challenges

Biotricity Inc. is a Nevada-based company specializing in remote monitoring technologies within the preventative healthcare sector, focusing on developing and commercializing a suite of medical devices and software solutions.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ending September 30, 2025, Biotricity Inc. highlighted a notable increase in revenue, driven by both technology fees and device sales, despite ongoing challenges reflected in its net loss.

The company reported a revenue increase to $7.76 million for the six months ending September 30, 2025, up from $6.47 million in the same period in 2024. This growth was primarily fueled by technology fees and increased device sales. However, the company continues to face financial hurdles, with a net loss of $1.36 million for the six-month period, although this is an improvement from the $5.26 million loss in the previous year. Biotricity’s operating expenses decreased, contributing to a positive operating profit of $555,764, compared to a loss in the prior year.

Despite the financial losses, Biotricity’s management remains optimistic about the company’s future, focusing on revenue growth and liquidity improvements through business development and potential equity and debt capitalizations. The company aims to continue its investment in research and development to enhance its product offerings and market reach.

Looking ahead, Biotricity plans to maintain its growth trajectory by leveraging its innovative healthcare solutions and expanding its market presence, while addressing its financial challenges through strategic initiatives.

data.title: Sigma Lithium Reports Strong Revenue Growth in Q3 2025
data.description:
Sigma Lithium Corporation ( (SGML) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Sigma Lithium Corporation presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Sigma Lithium Corporation is a leading global producer of lithium, focusing on sustainable production for electric vehicle batteries and energy storage systems, operating one of the largest lithium production sites in Brazil. In its latest earnings report for the third quarter of 2025, Sigma Lithium announced a significant increase in net revenue, rising by 69% quarter-over-quarter and 36% year-over-year, attributed to strategic partnerships and effective management of lithium price fluctuations. The company generated $24 million from final price settlements and anticipates an additional $4 million from incremental settlements, alongside $33 million expected from the sale of high-purity lithium materials.

Key financial metrics revealed a 21% increase in sales volume, despite a 36% decrease in production volume compared to the previous quarter. The average net realized price per tonne increased by 40% quarter-over-quarter, contributing to the revenue growth. Sigma Lithium also reported a significant improvement in its cash position, converting trade receivables into $21 million in cash and benefiting from an $8 million increase in settled trade receivables.

The company is set to restart mining operations by the end of November, with a full ramp-up expected by the first quarter of 2026, supported by upgraded equipment and direct management of operations. Sigma Lithium has also made strides in deleveraging, reducing its short-term trade finance debt by 48% throughout the year.

Looking ahead, Sigma Lithium remains focused on expanding its production capacity and maintaining financial discipline. The management’s outlook suggests continued strategic growth and operational efficiency, positioning the company well in the competitive lithium market.

data.title: BrainStorm Cell Therapeutics Reports Q3 2025 Financials and Updates
data.description:
Brainstorm Cell Therapeutics ( (BCLI) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Brainstorm Cell Therapeutics presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

BrainStorm Cell Therapeutics Inc. is a pioneering company in the biotechnology sector, focusing on the development of adult stem cell therapies for neurodegenerative diseases. The company’s proprietary NurOwn® platform is designed to deliver targeted biological signals to modulate neuroinflammation and promote neuroprotection.

In its latest earnings report for the third quarter of 2025, BrainStorm Cell Therapeutics announced significant progress in its clinical trials and financial performance. The company is preparing to initiate a Phase 3b study of its lead investigational therapy, NurOwn, for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), following clearance from the U.S. FDA. This study aims to provide confirmatory data to support a potential Biologics License Application (BLA).

Key financial highlights from the report include a reduction in net loss, which stood at approximately $2.1 million for the quarter, compared to $2.7 million in the same period last year. The company’s general and administrative expenses also decreased significantly, from $2.0 million in the third quarter of 2024 to $1.1 million in 2025. However, the company’s cash position remains a concern, with cash and cash equivalents totaling only $0.23 million as of September 30, 2025.

Additionally, BrainStorm is advancing its clinical programs with the upcoming Phase 3b ENDURANCE study, expected to enroll around 200 participants. This trial will further assess the long-term safety and efficacy of NurOwn in ALS patients. The company is also engaging with the ALS community, acknowledging a Citizen Petition filed with the FDA, which highlights the ongoing interest in NurOwn’s potential therapeutic benefits.

Looking ahead, BrainStorm Cell Therapeutics remains committed to advancing its clinical programs and stabilizing its financial situation. The management is optimistic about the potential of NurOwn to become a valuable treatment for ALS, pending regulatory approval, and continues to work closely with clinical sites and manufacturing partners to ensure operational readiness.

Movano Inc., a technology company in the healthcare sector, is developing a platform to deliver medical-grade data through consumer health devices. data.title: ImmuCell Reports Q3 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Immucell ( (ICCC) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Immucell presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

ImmuCell Corporation, a company specializing in animal health products for dairy and beef cattle, has released its unaudited financial results for the third quarter of 2025. The company is known for its First Defense® product and is developing Re-Tain®, a treatment for mastitis in dairy cows.

In the latest earnings report, ImmuCell reported a decrease in product sales by 8% for the third quarter of 2025 compared to the same period in 2024. However, over the nine-month period ending September 30, 2025, the company saw a 7% increase in product sales, and a 16% increase over the twelve-month period. The company also achieved a net income of $1.8 million for the nine-month period, a significant improvement from a net loss of $2.7 million in the previous year.

Key financial metrics highlighted in the report include a recovery in gross margin to 42.6% for the nine-month period, up from 27.3% in 2024. The company also reported an adjusted EBITDA of $4.4 million for the nine-month period, a substantial increase from $35,000 in the same period last year. Despite a net loss of $140,000 in the third quarter, this was an improvement from a loss of $702,000 in the previous year.

Looking ahead, ImmuCell’s management remains optimistic about the company’s future. With sufficient inventory and a clean slate, the company is poised for product adoption and revenue growth. The ongoing development and market feedback collection for Re-Tain® also indicate potential future advancements in their product offerings.

data.title: Auxly Cannabis Group Reports Strong Q3 2025 Growth
data.description:
Auxly Cannabis Group (OTC) ( (CBWTF) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Auxly Cannabis Group (OTC) presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions

Auxly Cannabis Group Inc., a prominent player in the Canadian cannabis market, specializes in consumer packaged goods and is recognized for its innovative approach and strong brand presence. In its latest earnings report for the third quarter of 2025, Auxly reported significant growth in financial metrics, showcasing a robust performance in the cannabis sector. The company achieved net revenues of $39.9 million, marking a 20% increase from the previous year, and a net income surge of 533% to $20.5 million. Auxly’s gross margin on finished cannabis inventory sold improved to 56%, and its adjusted EBITDA rose by 48% to $12.3 million, reflecting enhanced operational efficiency and cost control.

Auxly’s strategic positioning in the Canadian market is underscored by its status as the third-largest licensed producer by market share, with its brand Back Forty leading the cannabis segment. The company also dominated the all-in-one vape category and held several top-selling SKUs nationwide. These achievements were driven by product innovation, increased capacity utilization, and favorable pricing strategies.

The company’s financial health is further highlighted by an 82% increase in cash flow from operations, reaching $11.0 million, and a cash reserve of $30.5 million by the end of the quarter. Auxly’s management attributes this success to its disciplined cost management and strategic focus on innovation, which has strengthened its market position and brand leadership.

Looking ahead, Auxly remains committed to sustaining profitable growth through continued innovation and operational excellence. The company plans to leverage its strong balance sheet to explore organic growth opportunities and enhance its market leadership. With a focus on efficiency and strategic investments, Auxly aims to drive long-term shareholder value and capitalize on emerging opportunities in both domestic and international markets.

data.title: Ensysce Biosciences Reports Increased R&D Expenses Amid Net Loss
data.description:
Ensysce Biosciences, Inc. ( (ENSC) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Ensysce Biosciences, Inc. presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Ensysce Biosciences, Inc. is a clinical-stage biotech company focused on developing safer prescription drugs using its proprietary technology platforms, primarily in the realm of abuse and overdose-resistant pain medications. The company is headquartered in La Jolla, California, and operates within the pharmaceutical industry, with a unique focus on opioid abuse deterrence and overdose prevention technologies.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, Ensysce Biosciences highlighted a significant increase in operating expenses, which led to a net loss for the period. The company continues to focus on its research and development efforts, particularly in advancing its PF614 and PF614-MPAR product candidates, which are designed to combat opioid abuse and overdose.

The key financial metrics from the report indicate that Ensysce’s total operating expenses increased to $10.6 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $7.1 million in the same period in 2024. This increase is primarily attributed to heightened research and development activities. Despite receiving federal grants amounting to $3.2 million, the company reported a net loss of $7.4 million for the nine-month period. Ensysce also engaged in several financing activities, including public offerings and warrant exercises, to support its ongoing operations.

Looking ahead, Ensysce Biosciences remains committed to its strategic focus on developing its proprietary drug technologies. The management anticipates that continued investment in research and development will be crucial for achieving future growth and addressing the challenges associated with opioid abuse. The company is also exploring additional funding opportunities to ensure the advancement of its product pipeline and sustain its operations.

HORIBA, Ltd. is a Japanese company specializing in the production of precision instruments and systems for a variety of industries, including automotive, environmental, medical, and semiconductor sectors. Known for its innovative solutions, HORIBA operates on a global scale, providing essential tools for scientific research and industrial applications.

In its latest earnings report for the nine months ending September 30, 2025, HORIBA, Ltd. reported a positive financial performance with significant growth in key metrics. The company achieved net sales of 230,245 million yen, marking a 6.7% increase from the previous year. Operating income rose by 12.1% to 33,515 million yen, and net income attributable to shareholders increased by 21.0% to 24,096 million yen.

The report highlights robust performance across its business segments, with the Energy & Environment division experiencing a notable 10.0% increase in net sales. The Materials & Semiconductor segment also showed a healthy growth of 5.4%. Despite challenges in the Bio & Healthcare sector, which saw a modest 2.4% rise, the overall strategic realignment of business segments appears to be yielding positive results.

Looking ahead, HORIBA’s management remains optimistic, forecasting continued growth for the full year ending December 31, 2025. The company anticipates net sales to reach 335,000 million yen, with operating income projected to increase by 7.6% to 52,000 million yen. This outlook reflects HORIBA’s strategic focus on innovation and expansion in key markets, positioning the company for sustained success in the coming quarters.

Quantum Computing Inc. (QCi) is an innovative company specializing in integrated photonics and quantum optics technology, offering accessible quantum machines and foundry services for high-performance computing, AI, and cybersecurity applications.

In its third-quarter 2025 earnings report, QCi announced significant revenue growth and strategic advancements. The company reported a 280% increase in revenue year-over-year, driven by rising demand for its quantum products and services. The company’s assets surged to $898.2 million, bolstered by successful private placements raising $1.25 billion, enhancing its cash and investment positions.

Operationally, QCi made strides in quantum AI and cybersecurity solutions, securing a significant order from a major U.S. bank. The company also advanced its partnership with NASA, applying its quantum technology to space-based data challenges. Additionally, QCi focused on expanding its photonic chip foundry capabilities and industry engagement through conferences and strategic hiring.

Looking ahead, QCi remains committed to scaling its quantum and photonic manufacturing capabilities, leveraging its strong financial position to drive long-term growth and meet global demand for quantum technologies.

data.title: Benitec Biopharma Reports Increased Quarterly Loss Amid Rising Expenses
data.description:
Benitec Biopharma ( (BNTC) ) has released its Q1 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Benitec Biopharma presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Benitec Biopharma Inc. is a biotechnology company specializing in the development of genetic medicines using its proprietary ‘Silence and Replace’ DNA-directed RNA interference platform, which combines RNA interference with gene therapy to target disease-causing genes. In its recent earnings report for the quarter ending September 30, 2025, Benitec Biopharma reported a net loss of $8.965 million, an increase from the $5.059 million loss in the same quarter of the previous year. The company’s operating expenses rose significantly to $9.803 million, driven by increased general and administrative costs. Tivic Health Systems, Inc. is a diversified immunotherapeutics company focused on leveraging biopharmaceutical and bioelectronic systems to address unmet medical needs, particularly in the field of immunotherapy. The company is advancing its biologics pipeline, including its lead drug candidate Entolimod for acute radiation syndrome.

In the third quarter of 2025, Tivic Health Systems reported financial results highlighting strategic transformations and advancements in its biologics pipeline. The company is progressing towards commercial readiness, with significant developments in manufacturing processes and clinical trials.

Looking ahead, Tivic Health Systems remains committed to advancing its clinical pipeline and exploring potential pathways for deploying Entolimod as a military countermeasure. Despite the revenue dip, the company reported a 7% improvement in direct operating margin, reaching 58%, and highlighted strong performance in ancillary markets for its recent release, ‘The Toxic Avenger Unrated.’

The company’s financial performance was marked by a significant increase in SG&A expenses, up by 79% to $11.4 million, primarily driven by marketing costs for ‘The Toxic Avenger Unrated’ and investments in expanding its theatrical slate and technology capabilities. The net loss attributable to common stockholders widened to $5.7 million compared to $1.4 million in the prior-year quarter, while adjusted EBITDA turned negative at $3.7 million from a positive $0.5 million last year. Despite these challenges, Cineverse continues to expand its content offerings and strategic partnerships, including a new venture for microseries and a re-release of ‘Pan’s Labyrinth.’

Cineverse’s operational developments include the launch of a new studio platform, MicroCo, and the expansion of its technology leadership team. The company also announced several strategic partnerships and acquisitions, such as the US rights for ‘Air Bud Returns’ and a partnership with Liquid Death for ‘The Toxic Avenger Unrated.’ These initiatives are part of Cineverse’s strategy to leverage its extensive content library and technology platforms to drive future growth.

Looking ahead, Cineverse remains focused on enhancing its technology and content distribution capabilities, with plans to release the 20th anniversary edition of ‘Pan’s Labyrinth’ and further develop its Matchpoint™ technology partnerships. The company aims to maintain its growth trajectory by balancing strategic investments with financial discipline, positioning itself as a leader in both technology and entertainment sectors.

data.title: BIO-key International Reports Q3 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Bio-Key International ( (BKYI) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Bio-Key International presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

BIO-key International, Inc. is a leading provider of Identity and Access Management (IAM) and biometric authentication technologies, offering solutions that secure access for over forty million users globally. The company operates primarily in the cybersecurity sector, focusing on biometric-centric, multi-factor identity solutions.

In its latest earnings report for the third quarter of 2025, BIO-key International reported a decline in total revenues to $1.55 million, down from $2.14 million in the same period last year. The decrease was attributed to the absence of large orders from key customers that had boosted the previous year’s results. Despite this, the company highlighted strategic advancements, including the launch of its EcoID III fingerprint scanner and a significant new deployment in the Middle East defense sector.

Key financial metrics revealed a 7.8% reduction in operating expenses, driven by cost-cutting measures, although the company still reported a net loss of $964,849 for the quarter. The gross margin remained strong at 77%, and the company raised $3.1 million in gross proceeds from a warrant exercise agreement, which is expected to support future growth initiatives.

BIO-key’s strategic focus includes expanding its presence in Europe and the Middle East, leveraging its CyberDefense Initiative to capitalize on increased defense spending in these regions. The company is also enhancing its product offerings with advanced biometric solutions, aiming to capture opportunities in defense, finance, government, and higher education sectors.

Looking ahead, BIO-key anticipates a strong finish to 2025 with projected full-year revenues between $6.5 million and $7 million. The management remains optimistic about future growth, driven by strategic partnerships and a focus on cost reduction to achieve positive cash flow and profitability.

data.title: Alithya Group Reports Strong Revenue Growth Amid Challenges
data.description:
Alithya Group, Inc. Class A ( (ALYAF) ) has released its Q2 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Alithya Group, Inc. Class A presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Alithya Group, Inc. Class A is a strategic consulting and digital transformation services company that leverages AI and the latest technologies to solve business challenges and unlock new opportunities for its clients. The company operates in the technology sector and is known for its specialized global delivery network and AI-based IP solutions.

In its latest earnings report for the second quarter of fiscal 2026, Alithya Group reported significant growth in revenues and gross margins. The company achieved an 11.5% increase in revenues, reaching $124.3 million, and a 25.5% increase in gross margin, which rose to $42.8 million. Despite these positive results, the company reported a net loss of $31.0 million due to a $38.0 million impairment charge.

Key financial metrics highlighted in the report include an 80% increase in Adjusted Net Earnings to $9.5 million and a 37.5% rise in Adjusted EBITDA to $12.8 million. The company’s U.S. revenues saw a substantial increase of 34.8%, driven by organic growth and recent acquisitions. However, Canadian revenues declined due to reduced government contracts and project maturities.

Looking forward, Alithya’s management remains optimistic about the company’s growth prospects. They emphasize the company’s expanded capabilities through acquisitions and integration of new technologies, positioning Alithya to undertake larger, high-value projects essential to AI transformation. The company continues to focus on value creation for clients and steady growth in the industry.
